concepts.finding: Learnability requires systematic X-to-Y relationship estimable despite noise. Y = f(X) + epsilon. Must have regularity discoverable via hypothesis space search.
concepts.implication: Shopping intent learnable only if structured regularities tied to purchase goals exist in behavioral data.
concepts.open_question: Can shopping intent be reliably separated from browsing noise using only behavioral signals?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: Purchase sessions exhibit systematic behavioral patterns not present in non-purchase sessions.

concepts.finding: Generalization requires train/test from same distribution. Stationarity: past persists. IID: independent samples from same joint distribution.
concepts.implication: Seasonal/trend shifts break distribution assumptions. Intent models must be retrained as behavior evolves.
concepts.open_question: How can distribution shift in real-time query streams be detected without ground-truth labels?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: Wasserstein distance between weekly query embeddings predicts clustering quality degradation.

concepts.finding: Dense embeddings share statistical strength across similar features, enabling generalization to unseen events. Sparse-to-dense shift finds combinations without manual engineering.
concepts.implication: Embedding-based query understanding superior to sparse methods via semantic similarity capture.
concepts.open_question: What dimensionality optimally balances intent discrimination and generalization for short queries?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: 384d MiniLM embeddings produce more coherent intent clusters than TF-IDF for short queries.

concepts.finding: Construct validity: does operational target variable represent the intended higher-order concept? Many constructs lack natural units.
concepts.implication: Intent is latent with no ground-truth units. CTR/conversion are proxies, not direct measures. Must validate operationalization.
concepts.open_question: What behavioral signature best validates that queries share the same underlying intent?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: Human-labeled intent clusters show higher coherence on behavioral metrics than lexical similarity clusters.

concepts.finding: No model is neutral. Inductive biases encode assumptions. No Free Lunch: no single method dominates all datasets.
concepts.implication: HDBSCAN, k-means encode different assumptions about cluster shape and density. Evaluate multiple algorithms.
concepts.open_question: Which clustering algorithm's inductive bias best matches search query intent space structure?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: HDBSCAN produces more interpretable intent clusters than k-means due to non-uniform density of intent space.

concepts.finding: Correlation does not prove causation. ML models observational distributions, not causal ones.
concepts.implication: Query-click correlations may reflect position bias confounding rather than true intent. Causal methods needed.
concepts.open_question: How much query-click correlation is causal vs. confounded by SERP layout?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: Debiased click models produce significantly different intent labels than naive CTR aggregation.

concepts.finding: Inus conditions: insufficient but non-redundant parts of unnecessary but sufficient conditions. Context-dependent probability increases.
concepts.implication: No single query feature is necessary/sufficient for intent. Emerges from combined weak indicators.
concepts.open_question: What minimal feature set is sufficient for reliable intent classification?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: Intent accuracy plateaus beyond 10 carefully chosen features.

concepts.finding: LLM 'understanding' is behavioral proxy. Post-hoc explanations may not reflect actual internal logic. CoT paths may be unfaithful.
concepts.implication: LLM query labels may be unfaithful post-hoc rationalizations rather than accurate decisions.
concepts.open_question: Can LLM-generated intent labels be trusted for training downstream models?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: LLM intent labels for ambiguous queries show low inter-rater reliability across prompting strategies.

concepts.finding: Training: constrained parameter search minimizing loss via gradient descent. Overparameterized models generalize via regularization + distributed representations.
concepts.implication: Large embedding models may overfit to noisy behavioral data. Regularization critical.
concepts.open_question: What is optimal training signal for query embeddings: clicks, relevance judgments, or semantic similarity?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: Contrastive learning on query-click pairs outperforms fine-tuning on semantic similarity alone.

concepts.finding: Curse of dimensionality: data needs grow exponentially with features. Non-parametric methods fail in high-d.
concepts.implication: 768d+ embeddings may hurt clustering via distance concentration. UMAP/PCA essential.
concepts.open_question: What minimum dimension preserves query intent structure for clustering?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: UMAP to 5-10d improves HDBSCAN quality over full 768d embeddings.

concepts.finding: Useful representation preserves latent relationships. Deep learning disentangles factors of variation.
concepts.implication: Good query embeddings should disentangle topical, transactional, navigational intent facets separately.
concepts.open_question: Should query representations be multi-vector for different intent dimensions?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: Multi-vector representations (topic + intent type + entity) enable finer-grained clustering.

concepts.finding: Learning as abstraction = removing irrelevant details to find generalized rules = compression. MDL: best hypothesis gives max compression.
concepts.implication: Intent categories are compressed behavioral pattern abstractions. Good taxonomies maximize compression.
concepts.open_question: What category count balances compression and discriminative power?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: MDL-derived taxonomies align well with human-curated intent taxonomies.

concepts.finding: Interpretability = degree of estimable cause-effect relationships within model. White-box vs black-box distinction.
concepts.implication: Linear probes on frozen embeddings preferred over black-box deep classifiers for production debugging.
concepts.open_question: How much accuracy must be sacrificed for interpretability in intent classification?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: Linear classifiers on frozen embeddings achieve >90% of deep model accuracy for broad intent categories.

concepts.finding: Irreducible error from unmeasured variables cannot be eliminated. Minimum Bayes error due to stochasticity.
concepts.implication: Some queries are genuinely ambiguous and cannot be perfectly classified regardless of model quality.
concepts.open_question: What fraction of queries have truly ambiguous intent?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: Inter-annotator agreement plateaus at 85-90%, bounding achievable accuracy.

concepts.finding: Robustness via perturbation and invariance tests. Neural nets are excessively linear -> sensitive to adversarial inputs.
concepts.implication: Intent classifiers should be tested: meaning-preserving phrasing variations should not change predictions.
concepts.open_question: What perturbations preserve vs. change query intent?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: Synonym-substituted queries produce more consistent labels from embedding vs. lexical classifiers.

concepts.finding: Concept drift reveals intelligence tied to training distribution. Production deviation exposes invalid assumptions.
concepts.implication: Intent patterns drift ('Apple' fruit->tech). Models need continuous monitoring and periodic retraining.
concepts.open_question: How frequently do query intent distributions shift enough to degrade performance?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: Quarterly retraining maintains accuracy within 2% of continuously retrained models.
